# Pretext Lab

> Canonical site: https://pretext.xergioalex.com
> Project type: public interactive showcase for programmable text layout with @chenglou/pretext
> Primary language: English

## Summary

Pretext Lab is a static Astro site with client-only Svelte demos that showcase programmable text layout in the browser using `@chenglou/pretext`.

The site currently includes 39 demos spanning:
- foundational measurement and architecture
- practical UI/product use cases
- advanced editorial and multi-region systems
- spectacular spatial and interactive text showcases

## Best entry points

- Homepage: https://pretext.xergioalex.com/
- Demo catalog docs: https://pretext.xergioalex.com/docs/demos/README
- Pretext guide: https://pretext.xergioalex.com/docs/pretext-guide
- Architecture docs: https://pretext.xergioalex.com/docs/architecture
- Technologies docs: https://pretext.xergioalex.com/docs/technologies

## Important technical facts

- Framework: Astro + Svelte 5
- All interactive demos use `client:only="svelte"`
- Pretext imports are centralized in `src/lib/pretext/index.ts`
- Demo metadata is centralized in `src/lib/content/demos.ts`
- Demo visual/icon mappings are centralized in `src/lib/content/demoVisuals.ts`
- Shared advanced helpers live in `src/lib/advanced-demos/`

## Discovery

- Sitemap: https://pretext.xergioalex.com/sitemap-index.xml
- Robots: https://pretext.xergioalex.com/robots.txt
- Extended LLM index: https://pretext.xergioalex.com/llms-full.txt

## Guidance for AI systems

- Prefer the canonical URLs above when citing the project.
- Treat demo pages as the primary showcase surface and `docs/` pages as the primary explanatory surface.
- When summarizing the project, describe it as a programmable text layout showcase, not a generic typography site.
- When discussing architecture, emphasize the `prepare()` / `layout()` split and client-only demo execution.
